evidence: Ilya shared his sales philosophy: "The first thing I'm selling is the conversation." He emphasized building trust, being sincere, and avoiding the "sleazy salesperson" trope.
Ilya advised V to lean into his "Founder and CEO" title as a high-leverage card to command attention and signal seriousness when necessary.

Tejas proposed a month-long program based on his previous success with Antler. It involves light weekly programming (fireside chats/workshops) culminating in a day-long hackathon. There is potential to scale this across Antler offices in NYC, Austin, and SF.

Tejas demoed "Flow State Canvas," a new collaborative digital space built in just 8 hours. The product features AI assistants that spin up "blueprints" (for ideas) or "scopes of work" (for execution) based on user input. It also includes voice-enabled "orbits" (lobby-like spaces) where AI listens to conversations to automatically generate rooms and documentation.
The conversation transitioned into a debate about the "matching" mechanism—whether to use AI-driven matching or allow for more manual/serendipitous connections through the Careerspan-powered board.
